test_cases.name: Good-match score is at least 0.75
test_cases.given: A developer with high skill overlap (Python/FastAPI/PostgreSQL/Docker/React, 8yr exp), aligned work-style ([0.9,0.8,...]), compatible timezone (Europe/Warsaw), and matching career goals
test_cases.when: compute_match_score() is called with default weights (w1=0.30, w2=0.25, w3=0.20, w4=0.15, w5=0.10) against a Python/FastAPI/PostgreSQL project in UTC+1 to UTC+3
test_cases.then: match_score >= 0.75 confirming the labeled good-match scenario

test_cases.name: Bad-match score is at most 0.45
test_cases.given: A developer with zero skill overlap (Java/Spring), opposing work-style ([0.1]*8), misaligned timezone (US/Pacific), and non-matching career goals
test_cases.when: compute_match_score() is called with default weights against a Python/FastAPI project requiring UTC+5 to UTC+8
test_cases.then: match_score <= 0.45 confirming the labeled bad-match scenario

test_cases.name: Behavioral dimension (w2) is non-zero and active
test_cases.given: Two profile pairs with identical skills; Pair A has opposing work_style ([0.1]*8) and Pair B has aligned work_style ([0.9]*8)
test_cases.when: match_score is computed for both pairs
test_cases.then: Pair A score is strictly lower than Pair B score, with difference >= 0.10

test_cases.name: Stub explanation contains skill, behavioral, and growth sections
test_cases.given: A good-match developer and project profile pair with skill_score=0.80, workstyle_score=0.75, motivation_score=0.70, growth_score=0.65
test_cases.then: Explanation string has len >= 50 and contains keywords: 'skill', any of ('behavioral','work-style','work style'), any of ('growth','career','potential')

test_cases.name: Stub explanation valid with zero skill overlap
test_cases.given: A bad-match profile pair with skill_score=0.0
test_cases.then: Explanation is a valid string >= 50 chars with all three required sections

test_cases.name: match_score is a float bounded in [0.0, 1.0]
test_cases.given: Various component score combinations including all-zeros, all-ones, and midpoints
test_cases.then: Result is a float in [0.0, 1.0] for all inputs

test_cases.name: Weight change causes deterministic score delta
test_cases.given: A bad-match profile pair with skill_score~=0.0 and moderate behavioral scores
test_cases.when: compute_match_score() is called with default weights and then with skill-heavy weights (w1=0.70)
test_cases.then: The score difference between default and skill-heavy is >= 0.04

test_cases.name: Skill score is 0.0 when no skill overlap
test_cases.given: Developer has Java/Spring skills; project requires Python/FastAPI

test_cases.name: Skill score is near maximum with exact skill match
test_cases.given: Developer has exactly the required skills and 10 years experience

test_cases.name: Skill score increases with experience years
test_cases.given: Same skill set with 0 vs 10 years experience

test_cases.name: Skill score is always bounded in [0.0, 1.0]
test_cases.given: Developer with more skills than required and 15 years experience

test_cases.name: Timezone score is 1.0 when developer is within required range
test_cases.given: Developer timezone is Europe/Warsaw (UTC+1/UTC+2); project requires UTC+1 to UTC+3

test_cases.name: Timezone score is 0.0 when developer is far outside required range
test_cases.given: Developer is US/Pacific (UTC-8); project requires UTC+5 to UTC+8

test_cases.name: Timezone score is partial for moderate timezone distance
test_cases.given: Developer is US/Eastern (UTC-5/-4); project requires UTC+0 to UTC+3

test_cases.name: Growth score is high when goals match opportunities
test_cases.given: Developer goals: ['technical leadership', 'distributed systems']; project opportunities overlap

test_cases.name: Growth score is low when goals do not match
test_cases.given: Developer goal: 'legacy systems'; project opportunities: kubernetes/microservices/ML

test_cases.name: Growth score is 0.0 when career_goals is empty
test_cases.given: Developer has no career_goals

test_cases.name: Cosine similarity of identical vectors is 1.0
test_cases.given: Two identical vectors v

test_cases.name: Cosine similarity of opposite vectors is 0.0
test_cases.given: v1=[1,1,1] and v2=[-1,-1,-1] (opposing vectors)

test_cases.name: Cosine similarity returns 0.5 (neutral) for zero-norm vector
test_cases.given: v1=[0,0,0] (zero vector) and any non-zero v2

test_cases.name: Pure computation latency is under 500ms at p95
test_cases.given: 100 iterations of the full five-dimension score computation plus stub explanation
test_cases.when: timing is measured for all 100 iterations

test_cases.name: Match score is 0.0 when all component scores are 0.0 (negative test)
test_cases.given: All component scores are 0.0

test_cases.name: Match score does not exceed 1.0 with overflowing inputs
test_cases.given: w1=1.0, all other weights 0.0, all scores 1.0

test_cases.name: Derived project work-style vector has exactly 8 dimensions
test_cases.given: Any team_structure, workload_intensity, innovation_level

test_cases.name: Prompt artifact is a versioned file, not inline code
test_cases.given: The artifacts/prompts/match_explanation_v1.json artifact file exists
test_cases.when: The file is loaded and ClaudeService is initialized
test_cases.then: File contains required fields (prompt_key, version, template_text, model_name); ClaudeService.prompt.template_text matches the file content

test_suites.name: Risk Engine Unit Tests (Burnout and Bench Risk)

test_cases.name: Burnout risk > 0.6 after 48+ consecutive weeks at intensity >= 0.8
test_cases.given: A developer with one AllocationSlice spanning ~52 weeks at workload_intensity=0.8, motivation_alignment_factor=0.0

test_cases.name: Burnout risk > 0.6 for exactly 48 weeks at intensity 0.9
test_cases.given: AllocationSlice of exactly 48 weeks at workload_intensity=0.9

test_cases.name: Burnout risk < 0.6 for fewer than 48 consecutive weeks
test_cases.given: AllocationSlice of ~16 weeks at workload_intensity=0.85

test_cases.name: Burnout risk is 0.0 with full motivation alignment
test_cases.given: Long high-intensity allocation but motivation_alignment_factor=1.0

test_cases.name: Burnout risk is 0.0 when there are no allocations
test_cases.given: Empty allocation list

test_cases.name: Burnout risk is 0.0 for low-intensity allocations (< 0.8 threshold)
test_cases.given: AllocationSlice spanning 60+ weeks at workload_intensity=0.79

test_cases.name: Bench risk > 0.7 when project ends in 7 days with no follow-on
test_cases.given: Active allocation ending in 7 days, no follow-on allocation

test_cases.name: Bench risk is very high when project ends tomorrow
test_cases.given: Active allocation ending in 1 day, no follow-on allocation

test_cases.name: Bench risk is 0.0 when follow-on allocation starts within 28 days of project end
test_cases.given: Current allocation ends in 7 days; follow-on starts 5 days later

test_cases.name: Bench risk is 1.0 when developer has no active allocation
test_cases.given: Only a past (inactive) allocation

test_cases.name: Bench risk is 0.0 when project ends far in the future
test_cases.given: Active allocation ending in 20 weeks

test_cases.name: Burnout badge maps correctly for score 0.0 (low)
test_cases.given: burnout_risk_score = 0.0

test_cases.name: Burnout badge maps correctly for score 0.39 (low)
test_cases.given: burnout_risk_score = 0.39

test_cases.name: Burnout badge maps correctly for score 0.4 (medium)
test_cases.given: burnout_risk_score = 0.4

test_cases.name: Burnout badge maps correctly for score 0.6 (medium)
test_cases.given: burnout_risk_score = 0.6

test_cases.name: Burnout badge maps correctly for score 0.61 (high)
test_cases.given: burnout_risk_score = 0.61

test_cases.name: Burnout badge maps correctly for score 1.0 (high)
test_cases.given: burnout_risk_score = 1.0

test_cases.name: Bench badge maps correctly for score 0.0 (low)
test_cases.given: bench_risk_score = 0.0

test_cases.name: Bench badge maps correctly for score 0.39 (low)
test_cases.given: bench_risk_score = 0.39

test_cases.name: Bench badge maps correctly for score 0.4 (medium)
test_cases.given: bench_risk_score = 0.4

test_cases.name: Bench badge maps correctly for score 0.7 (medium)
test_cases.given: bench_risk_score = 0.7

test_cases.name: Bench badge maps correctly for score 0.71 (high)
test_cases.given: bench_risk_score = 0.71

test_cases.name: Bench badge maps correctly for score 1.0 (high)
test_cases.given: bench_risk_score = 1.0

test_cases.name: compute_risk_scores returns all required fields
test_cases.given: An AllocationSlice spanning ~52 weeks at intensity 0.85
test_cases.then: Result has: burnout_risk_score, bench_risk_score, burnout_risk_badge, bench_risk_badge, computed_at; both scores in [0.0,1.0]; both badges in {low,medium,high}

test_cases.name: compute_risk_scores with no allocations: burnout=0.0 (low), bench=1.0 (high)
test_cases.given: Empty allocation list
test_cases.then: burnout_risk_score == 0.0, bench_risk_score == 1.0, badges are 'low' and 'high' respectively

test_cases.name: GDPR DELETE returns HTTP 204 No Content
test_cases.given: An existing DeveloperProfile in the mock DB
test_cases.when: DELETE /api/v1/developers/{id} is called with manager auth

test_cases.name: Subsequent GET after GDPR erasure returns HTTP 404
test_cases.given: A developer profile that has been deleted

test_cases.name: GDPR audit log records all 6 cascade steps
test_cases.given: An existing DeveloperProfile
test_cases.then: ErasureAuditLog is created with status='completed' and steps_completed containing all 6 entity classes

test_cases.name: DELETE non-existent developer returns 404 (negative test)
test_cases.given: A developer ID that does not exist in the DB

test_cases.name: Developer cannot erase another developer's profile (negative test)
test_cases.given: Developer A attempts to delete Developer B's profile
test_cases.when: DELETE /api/v1/developers/{victim_id} is called with Developer A's JWT

test_cases.name: POST /matches/feedback stores record and returns HTTP 201
test_cases.given: An existing MatchRecord and a developer submitting feedback (accepted=false)
test_cases.when: POST /api/v1/matches/feedback is called with developer_id, match_id, accepted=false
test_cases.then: HTTP 201; FeedbackRecord is added to DB; response contains id, developer_id, match_id, accepted, feedback_timestamp

test_cases.name: GET /analytics/rejection-rate returns correct ratio with >= 10 samples
test_cases.given: 15 feedback records where 6 are rejections (rejected/total = 6/15 = 0.4)

test_cases.name: rejection_ratio is null when sample_count < minimum floor (10)
test_cases.given: 3 feedback records (below the floor of 10)

test_cases.name: POST /matches/feedback returns 404 for non-existent match (negative test)
test_cases.given: A match_id that does not exist in the DB

test_cases.name: POST /matches/feedback returns 422 when 'accepted' field is missing (negative test)
test_cases.given: A feedback request body without the required 'accepted' boolean

test_cases.name: GET /analytics/rejection-rate returns 422 without developer_id (negative test)
test_cases.given: GET request with no developer_id query parameter

test_cases.name: GET /config/weights returns the default weight configuration
test_cases.given: The singleton WeightConfig row with default values (w1=0.30, ..., w5=0.10)
test_cases.then: HTTP 200; response contains w1=0.30, w2=0.25, w3=0.20, w4=0.15, w5=0.10, version=1

test_cases.name: PUT /config/weights updates weights and increments version
test_cases.given: A manager submitting new valid weights (w1=0.40, w2=0.25, w3=0.20, w4=0.10, w5=0.05, sum=1.0)
test_cases.then: HTTP 200; response reflects updated weights; version incremented to 2

test_cases.name: Weight change causes deterministic score delta (API unit test)
test_cases.given: A developer with high skill/low behavioral scores against default and skill-heavy weights
test_cases.when: compute_match_score() is called with both weight sets

test_cases.name: PUT /config/weights returns 422 for sum != 1.0 (negative test)
test_cases.given: Weight payload where w1+w2+w3+w4+w5 = 1.05 (exceeds 1.0±0.001 tolerance)
test_cases.then: HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity (Pydantic model_validator rejects sum)

test_cases.name: PUT /config/weights returns 422 for negative weight (negative test)
test_cases.given: Weight payload with w1=-0.10 (violates Field(ge=0.0))

test_cases.name: PUT /config/weights by developer role returns 403 (negative test)
test_cases.given: A user with role='developer' (not manager) submitting valid weights

test_cases.name: PUT /config/weights returns 422 for weight > 1.0 (negative test)
test_cases.given: Weight payload with w1=1.10 (violates Field(le=1.0))

test_cases.name: POST /matches returns valid response schema with all required fields
test_cases.given: A complete DeveloperProfile (Python/FastAPI/PostgreSQL, Europe/Warsaw) and ProjectProfile mocked through dependency injection
test_cases.then: HTTP 201; response contains match_score in [0.0,1.0], explanation >= 50 chars, risks list, growth_potential list, component_scores, weights_snapshot, match_id, developer_id, project_id, created_at

test_cases.name: Match endpoint latency is under 500ms at p95
test_cases.given: 10 mock-DB POST /matches calls using dependency_overrides
test_cases.then: p95 latency < 500ms (algorithmic portion excluding Claude and live DB)

test_cases.name: Developer dashboard: GET /matches returns >= 1 project card with required fields
test_cases.given: A developer with at least one MatchRecord in the mock DB
test_cases.then: Response contains >= 1 match with match_score, explanation, risks, growth_potential, match_id, developer_id, project_id

test_cases.name: Manager risk endpoint returns badges without raw behavioral vectors
test_cases.given: A DeveloperProfile in the mock DB with no active allocations
test_cases.when: GET /api/v1/developers/{id}/risk is called with manager auth
test_cases.then: Response contains burnout_risk_score, bench_risk_score, burnout_risk_badge, bench_risk_badge; does NOT contain work_style_vector or motivation_vector

test_cases.name: Developer profile GET response excludes raw behavioral vectors
test_cases.given: A DeveloperProfile with work_style_vector and motivation_vector stored in DB
test_cases.then: Response contains id, skills, experience_years, timezone, embedding_status; does NOT contain work_style_vector or motivation_vector

test_cases.name: Full pipeline: profile ingest → score → explain → response
test_cases.given: A complete developer and project profile submitted as JSON with mock DB session
test_cases.when: POST /api/v1/matches executes the full algorithmic pipeline
test_cases.then: HTTP 201; match_score in [0.0,1.0]; explanation >= 50 chars; MatchRecord persisted to session; explanation_source='stub_pending'; no raw vectors in response

test_cases.name: End-to-end: create match then submit feedback
test_cases.given: A complete match is created via POST /matches; the resulting match_id is used in a feedback submission
test_cases.when: POST /matches/feedback is called with the match_id from the first request
test_cases.then: HTTP 201 for feedback; feedback body contains match_id, accepted=true, feedback_timestamp

test_cases.name: POST /matches returns 422 for missing required field (negative test)
test_cases.given: A match request where the developer_profile is missing the 'skills' array

test_cases.name: POST /matches returns 422 for work_style vector of wrong length (negative test)
test_cases.given: A match request where work_style has 5 elements instead of required 8

summary.coverage_note: Count regenerated 2026-06-28 by re-running the full suite (was 77 at the original recorded run; +31 from later operator-profile, team-mismatch, skill-alias, and profile-enrichment work). All 13 acceptance criteria (AC1–AC13) are covered by at least one test case. Good-match expected range: score >= 0.75 (TC-001). Bad-match expected range: score <= 0.45 (TC-002).
